children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to authorize, direct, facilitate, and expedite the exchange of land between Resolution Copper and the United States.

children.heading: Apache Leap
children.label: 1 Apache Leap
children.text: The term “Apache Leap” means the approximately 807 acres of land depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Apache Leap” and dated March 2011.

children.heading: Federal land
children.label: 2 Federal land
children.text: The term “Federal land” means the approximately 2,422 acres of land located in Pinal County, Arizona, depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Federal Parcel–Oak Flat” and dated March 2011.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 3 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Non-Federal land
children.label: 4 Non-Federal land
children.text: The term “non-Federal land” means the parcels of land owned by Resolution Copper that are described in subsection (d)(1) and, if necessary to equalize the land exchange under subsection (c), subsection (c)(5)(B)(i)(I).

children.heading: Oak Flat Campground
children.label: 5 Oak Flat Campground
children.text: The term “Oak Flat Campground” means the approximately 50 acres of land comprising approximately 16 developed campsites depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Oak Flat Campground” and dated March 2011.

children.heading: Oak Flat Withdrawal Area
children.label: 6 Oak Flat Withdrawal Area
children.text: The term “Oak Flat Withdrawal Area” means the approximately 760 acres of land depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Oak Flat Withdrawal Area” and dated March 2011.

children.heading: Resolution Copper
children.label: 7 Resolution Copper
children.text: The term “Resolution Copper” means Resolution Copper Mining, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, including any successor, assign, affiliate, member, or joint venturer of Resolution Copper Mining, LLC.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 8 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: State
children.label: 9 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Arizona.

children.heading: Town
children.label: 10 Town
children.text: The term “Town” means the incorporated town of Superior, Arizona.

children.heading: Resolution mine plan of operations
children.label: 11 Resolution mine plan of operations
children.text: The term “Resolution mine plan of operations” means the mine plan of operations submitted to the Secretary by Resolution Copper in November, 2013, including any amendments or supplements.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to the provisions of this section, if Resolution Copper offers to convey to the United States all right, title, and interest of Resolution Copper in and to the non-Federal land, the Secretary is authorized and directed to convey to Resolution Copper, all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the Federal land.

children.label: A
children.text: is acceptable to the Secretary, for land to be administered by the Forest Service and the Secretary of the Interior, for land to be administered by the Bureau of Land Management; and

children.label: B
children.text: conforms to the title approval standards of the Attorney General of the United States applicable to land acquisitions by the Federal Government.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall engage in government-to-government consultation with affected Indian tribes concerning issues of concern to the affected Indian tribes related to the land exchange.

children.label: i
children.text: address the concerns of the affected Indian tribes; and

children.label: ii
children.text: minimize the adverse effects on the affected Indian tribes resulting from mining and related activities on the Federal land conveyed to Resolution Copper under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary and Resolution Copper shall select an appraiser to conduct appraisals of the Federal land and non-Federal land in compliance with the requirements of section 254.9 of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations.December 19, 2014

children.label: I
children.text: the Uniform Appraisal Standards for Federal Land Acquisitions; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice.

children.label: I
children.text: for a period of 3 years beginning on the date of the approval by the Secretary of the final appraised value; or

children.label: II
children.text: at all, in accordance with section 254.14 of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation), after an exchange agreement is entered into by Resolution Copper and the Secretary.

children.heading: Improvements
children.label: iii Improvements
children.text: Any improvements made by Resolution Copper prior to entering into an exchange agreement shall not be included in the appraised value of the Federal land.

children.heading: Public review
children.label: iv Public review
children.text: Before consummating the land exchange under this section, the Secretary shall make the appraisals of the land to be exchanged (or a summary thereof) available for public review.

children.heading: Appraisal information
children.label: C Appraisal information
children.text: The appraisal prepared under this paragraph shall include a detailed income capitalization approach analysis of the market value of the Federal land which may be utilized, as appropriate, to determine the value of the Federal land, and shall be the basis for calculation of any payment under subsection (e).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The value of the Federal land and non-Federal land to be exchanged under this section shall be equal or shall be equalized in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Surplus of Federal land value
children.label: B Surplus of Federal land value
I
children.text: convey additional non-Federal land in the State to the Secretary or the Secretary of the Interior, consistent with the requirements of this section and subject to the approval of the applicable Secretary;

children.label: II
children.text: make a cash payment to the United States; or

children.label: III
children.text: use a combination of the methods described in subclauses (I) and (II), as agreed to by Resolution Copper, the Secretary, and the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Amount of payment
children.label: ii Amount of payment
children.text: The Secretary may accept a payment in excess of 25 percent of the total value of the land or interests conveyed, notwithstanding section 206(b) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ().43 U.S.C. 1716(b)

children.heading: Disposition and use of proceeds
children.label: iii Disposition and use of proceeds
children.text: Any amounts received by the United States under this subparagraph shall be deposited in the fund established under and shall be made available to the Secretary for the acquisition of land or interests in land in Region 3 of the Forest Service.section 484a of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the United States shall not make a payment to Resolution Copper to equalize the value; and

children.label: ii
children.text: except as provided in subsection (h), the surplus value of the non-Federal land shall be considered to be a donation by Resolution Copper to the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: if so requested by Resolution Copper, within 30 days of such request, a special use permit to carry out mineral exploration activities under the Oak Flat Withdrawal Area from existing drill pads located outside the Area, if the activities would not disturb the surface of the Area; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if so requested by Resolution Copper, within 90 days of such request, a special use permit to carry out mineral exploration activities within the Oak Flat Withdrawal Area (but not within the Oak Flat Campground), if the activities are conducted from a single exploratory drill pad which is located to reasonably minimize visual and noise impacts on the Campground.

children.heading: Conditions
children.label: B Conditions
children.text: Any activities undertaken in accordance with this paragraph shall be subject to such reasonable terms and conditions as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Termination
children.label: C Termination
children.text: The authorization for Resolution Copper to undertake mineral exploration activities under this paragraph shall remain in effect until the Oak Flat Withdrawal Area land is conveyed to Resolution Copper in accordance with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: associated with the land exchange and any environmental review document under paragraph (9); and

children.label: B
children.text: agreed to by the Secretary.

children.heading: Use of Federal land
children.label: 8 Use of Federal land
children.text: The Federal land to be conveyed to Resolution Copper under this section shall be available to Resolution Copper for mining and related activities subject to and in accordance with applicable Federal, State, and local laws pertaining to mining and related activities on land in private ownership.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, the Secretary shall carry out the land exchange in accordance with the requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Environmental analysis
children.label: B Environmental analysis
children.text: Prior to conveying Federal land under this section, the Secretary shall prepare a single environmental impact statement under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.), which shall be used as the basis for all decisions under Federal law related to the proposed mine and the Resolution mine plan of operations and any related major Federal actions significantly affecting the quality of the human environment, including the granting of any permits, rights-of-way, or approvals for the construction of associated power, water, transportation, processing, tailings, waste disposal, or other ancillary facilities.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Impacts on cultural and archeological resources
children.label: C Impacts on cultural and archeological resources
i
children.text: assess the effects of the mining and related activities on the Federal land conveyed to Resolution Copper under this section on the cultural and archeological resources that may be located on the Federal land; and

children.label: ii
children.text: identify measures that may be taken, to the extent practicable, to minimize potential adverse impacts on those resources, if any.

children.label: i
children.text: the land exchange; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the extraction of minerals in commercial quantities by Resolution Copper on or under the Federal land.

children.heading: Title transfer
children.label: 10 Title transfer
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date of publication of the final environmental impact statement, the Secretary shall convey all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the Federal land to Resolution Copper.

children.heading: Conveyance and management of non-Federal land
children.label: d Conveyance and management of non-Federal land
i
children.text: the approximately 147 acres of land located in Gila County, Arizona, depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–Turkey Creek” and dated March 2011;

children.label: ii
children.text: the approximately 148 acres of land located in Yavapai County, Arizona, depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–Tangle Creek” and dated March 2011;

children.label: iii
children.text: the approximately 149 acres of land located in Maricopa County, Arizona, depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–Cave Creek” and dated March 2011;

children.label: iv
children.text: the approximately 640 acres of land located in Coconino County, Arizona, depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–East Clear Creek” and dated March 2011; and

children.label: v
children.text: the approximately 110 acres of land located in Pinal County, Arizona, depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–Apache Leap South End” and dated March 2011; and

children.label: i
children.text: the approximately 3,050 acres of land located in Pinal County, Arizona, identified as “Lands to DOI” as generally depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–Lower San Pedro River” and dated ;July 6, 2011

children.label: ii
children.text: the approximately 160 acres of land located in Gila and Pinal Counties, Arizona, identified as “Lands to DOI” as generally depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–Dripping Springs” and dated ; andJuly 6, 2011

children.label: iii
children.text: the approximately 940 acres of land located in Santa Cruz County, Arizona, identified as “Lands to DOI” as generally depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Non-Federal Parcel–Appleton Ranch” and dated .July 6, 2011

children.heading: Land acquired by the Secretary
children.label: A Land acquired by the Secretary
I
children.text: become part of the national forest in which the land is located; and

children.label: II
children.text: be administered in accordance with the laws applicable to the National Forest System.

children.heading: Boundary revision
children.label: ii Boundary revision
children.text: On the acquisition of land by the Secretary under this section, the boundaries of the national forest shall be modified to reflect the inclusion of the acquired land.

children.heading: Land and Water Conservation Fund
children.label: iii Land and Water Conservation Fund
children.text: For purposes of sections 100506(c) and 200306 of title 54, the boundaries of a national forest in which land acquired by the Secretary is located shall be deemed to be the boundaries of that forest as in existence on .January 1, 1965

children.heading: Land acquired by the Secretary of the Interior
children.label: B Land acquired by the Secretary of the Interior

children.heading: San Pedro National Conservation Area1So in original. The word “Riparian” probably should precede “National”.1
children.label: i San Pedro National Conservation Area1So in original. The word “Riparian” probably should precede “National”.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The land acquired by the Secretary of the Interior under paragraph (1)(B)(i) shall be added to, and administered as part of, the San Pedro National Conservation Area in accordance with the laws (including regulations) applicable to the Conservation Area.1

children.heading: Management plan
children.label: II Management plan
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which the land is acquired, the Secretary of the Interior shall update the management plan for the San Pedro National Conservation Area to reflect the management requirements of the acquired land.1

children.heading: Dripping springs
children.label: ii Dripping springs
children.text: Land acquired by the Secretary of the Interior under paragraph (1)(B)(ii) shall be managed in accordance with the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.) and applicable land use plans.43 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Las Cienegas National Conservation Area
children.label: iii Las Cienegas National Conservation Area
children.text: Land acquired by the Secretary of the Interior under paragraph (1)(B)(iii) shall be added to, and administered as part of, the Las Cienegas National Conservation Area in accordance with the laws (including regulations) applicable to the Conservation Area.

children.heading: Value adjustment payment to United States
children.label: e Value adjustment payment to United States

children.heading: Report required
children.label: A Report required
children.text: As a condition of the land exchange under this section, Resolution Copper shall submit to the Secretary of the Interior an annual report indicating the quantity of locatable minerals produced during the preceding calendar year in commercial quantities from the Federal land conveyed to Resolution Copper under subsection (c). The first report is required to be submitted not later than February 15 of the first calendar year beginning after the date of commencement of production of valuable locatable minerals in commercial quantities from such Federal land. The reports shall be submitted February 15 of each calendar year thereafter.

children.heading: Sharing reports with State
children.label: B Sharing reports with State
children.text: The Secretary shall make each report received under subparagraph (A) available to the State.

children.heading: Report contents
children.label: C Report contents
children.text: The reports under subparagraph (A) shall comply with any recordkeeping and reporting requirements prescribed by the Secretary or required by applicable Federal laws in effect at the time of production.

children.heading: Payment on production
children.label: 2 Payment on production
children.text: If the cumulative production of valuable locatable minerals produced in commercial quantities from the Federal land conveyed to Resolution Copper under subsection (c) exceeds the quantity of production of locatable minerals from the Federal land used in the income capitalization approach analysis prepared under subsection (c)(4)(C), Resolution Copper shall pay to the United States, by not later than March 15 of each applicable calendar year, a value adjustment payment for the quantity of excess production at the same rate assumed for the income capitalization approach analysis prepared under subsection (c)(4)(C).

children.heading: State law unaffected
children.label: 3 State law unaffected
children.text: Nothing in this subsection modifies, expands, diminishes, amends, or otherwise affects any State law relating to the imposition, application, timing, or collection of a State excise or severance tax.

children.heading: Separate fund
children.label: A Separate fund
children.text: All funds paid to the United States under this subsection shall be deposited in a special fund established in the Treasury and shall be available, in such amounts as are provided in advance in appropriation Acts, to the Secretary and the Secretary of the Interior only for the purposes authorized by subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Authorized use
children.label: B Authorized use
children.text: Amounts in the special fund established pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall be used for maintenance, repair, and rehabilitation projects for Forest Service and Bureau of Land Management assets.
entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws;
location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and
disposition under the mineral leasing, mineral materials, and geothermal leasing laws.

children.heading: Apache Leap Special Management Area
children.label: g Apache Leap Special Management Area

children.heading: Designation
children.label: 1 Designation
children.text: To further the purpose of this section, the Secretary shall establish a special management area consisting of Apache Leap, which shall be known as the “Apache Leap Special Management Area” (referred to in this subsection as the “special management area”).

children.label: A
children.text: to preserve the natural character of Apache Leap;

children.label: B
children.text: to allow for traditional uses of the area by Native American people; and

children.label: C
children.text: to protect and conserve the cultural and archeological resources of the area.

children.heading: Surrender of mining and extraction rights
children.label: 3 Surrender of mining and extraction rights
children.text: As a condition of the land exchange under subsection (c), Resolution Copper shall surrender to the United States, without compensation, all rights held under the mining laws and any other law to commercially extract minerals under Apache Leap.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall manage the special management area in a manner that furthers the purposes described in paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: installation of seismic monitoring equipment on the surface and subsurface to protect the resources located within the special management area;

children.label: ii
children.text: installation of fences, signs, or other measures necessary to protect the health and safety of the public; and

children.label: iii
children.text: operation of an underground tunnel and associated workings, as described in the Resolution mine plan of operations, subject to any terms and conditions the Secretary may reasonably require.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Secretary, in consultation with affected Indian tribes, the Town, Resolution Copper, and other interested members of the public, shall prepare a management plan for the Apache Leap Special Management Area.December 19, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: protect the cultural, archaeological, or historical resources of Apache Leap, including permanent or seasonal closures of all or a portion of Apache Leap; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide access for recreation.

children.heading: Mining activities
children.label: 6 Mining activities
children.text: The provisions of this subsection shall not impose additional restrictions on mining activities carried out by Resolution Copper adjacent to, or outside of, the Apache Leap area beyond those otherwise applicable to mining activities on privately owned land under Federal, State, and local laws, rules and regulations.

children.heading: Conveyances to Town of Superior, Arizona
children.label: h Conveyances to Town of Superior, Arizona
A
children.text: Approximately 30 acres of land as depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Federal Parcel–Fairview Cemetery” and dated March 2011.

children.label: B
children.text: The reversionary interest and any reserved mineral interest of the United States in the approximately 265 acres of land located in Pinal County, Arizona, as depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Federal Reversionary Interest–Superior Airport” and dated March 2011.

children.label: C
children.text: The approximately 250 acres of land located in Pinal County, Arizona, as depicted on the map entitled “Southeast Arizona Land Exchange and Conservation Act of 2011–Federal Parcel–Superior Airport Contiguous Parcels” and dated March 2011.

children.heading: Payment
children.label: 2 Payment
children.text: The Town shall pay to the Secretary the market value for each parcel of land or interest in land acquired under this subsection, as determined by appraisals conducted in accordance with subsection (c)(4).

children.heading: Sisk Act
children.label: 3 Sisk Act
children.text: Any payment received by the Secretary from the Town under this subsection shall be deposited in the fund established under and shall be made available to the Secretary for the acquisition of land or interests in land in Region 3 of the Forest Service.section 484a of this title

children.heading: Terms and conditions
children.label: 4 Terms and conditions
children.text: The conveyances under this subsection shall be subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Revocation of orders
children.label: A Revocation of orders
children.text: Any public land order that withdraws the Federal land from appropriation or disposal under a public land law shall be revoked to the extent necessary to permit disposal of the land.

children.label: i
children.text: on the date of consummation of the land exchange; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if Resolution Copper notifies the Secretary in writing that it has elected to withdraw from the land exchange pursuant to section 206(d) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976, as amended ().43 U.S.C. 1716(d)

children.heading: Rights of Resolution Copper
children.label: C Rights of Resolution Copper
children.text: Nothing in this section shall interfere with, limit, or otherwise impair, the unpatented mining claims or rights currently held by Resolution Copper on the Federal land, nor in any way change, diminish, qualify, or otherwise impact Resolution Copper’s rights and ability to conduct activities on the Federal land under such unpatented mining claims and the general mining laws of the United States, including the permitting or authorization of such activities.

children.heading: Minor errors
children.label: A Minor errors
children.text: The Secretary concerned and Resolution Copper may correct, by mutual agreement, any minor errors in any map, acreage estimate, or description of any land conveyed or exchanged under this section.

children.heading: Conflict
children.label: B Conflict
children.text: If there is a conflict between a map, an acreage estimate, or a description of land in this section, the map shall control unless the Secretary concerned and Resolution Copper mutually agree otherwise.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: C Availability
children.text: On , the Secretary shall file and make available for public inspection in the Office of the Supervisor, Tonto National Forest, each map referred to in this section.December 19, 2014

children.heading: Public access in and around Oak Flat Campground
children.label: 3 Public access in and around Oak Flat Campground
children.text: As a condition of conveyance of the Federal land, Resolution Copper shall agree to provide access to the surface of the Oak Flat Campground to members of the public, including Indian tribes, to the maximum extent practicable, consistent with health and safety requirements, until such time as the operation of the mine precludes continued public access for safety reasons, as determined by Resolution Copper.
